name: PDE5 Class Transfer Framework Extract
population_or_context: Project-level framework for classifying tadalafil-direct, adjacent non-tadalafil PDE5, mixed PDE5, and class-level evidence.
methods: Rules derived from shared PDE5 mechanism, label-based pharmacokinetic differences, label-based safety boundaries, and comparative pharmacology review evidence.
population_profile: Intended for synthesis across healthy men age 30 to 50 and adjacent ED/BPH, cardiovascular, mood/well-being, exercise, and longevity-relevant evidence.
tadalafil_exposure: Framework keeps direct tadalafil evidence separate from sildenafil, vardenafil, avanafil, mixed PDE5, and unspecified class evidence.
class_transfer_limits: Tadalafil claims should use non-tadalafil PDE5 evidence only as adjacent plausibility or class-boundary support unless the synthesis explicitly justifies transfer across molecule, dose, frequency, timing, half-life, PDE selectivity, population, comparator, endpoint, and safety context.
indication_context: Evidence-directness framework for off-label review, not a treatment recommendation.
study_design: Curated framework artifact generated from staged baseline sources.
comparator_context: Compares evidence directness categories rather than treatment effects.
safety_findings: Framework requires safety caveats to travel with any adjacent PDE5 evidence, especially nitrate/GC-stimulator contraindications, hypotension interactions, CYP3A4 restrictions, agent-specific warnings, and tadalafil daily-exposure issues.
directness_to_target_population: Direct for project synthesis rules; indirect for any individual clinical outcome.
confounding_and_bias_limits: The framework does not prove interchangeability or class efficacy; it only defines how adjacent evidence may be labeled and limited.
